segments.text: Again the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,
Son of man, cause Jerusalem to know her abominations,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Thy birth and thy nativity is of the land of Canaan; thy father was an Amorite, and thy mother an Hittite.
segments.speaker: Lord GOD

footnotes: birth: Heb. cutting out, or, habitation

segments.text: And as for thy nativity, in the day thou wast born thy navel was not cut, neither wast thou washed in water to supple thee; thou wast not salted at all, nor swaddled at all.

footnotes: to supple…: or, when I looked upon thee

segments.text: None eye pitied thee, to do any of these unto thee, to have compassion upon thee; but thou wast cast out in the open field, to the lothing of thy person, in the day that thou wast born.
Live; yea, I said unto thee when thou wast in thy blood, Live.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I have caused thee to multiply as the bud of the field, and thou hast increased and waxen great, and thou art come to excellent ornaments: thy breasts are fashioned, and thine hair is grown, whereas thou wast naked and bare.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: caused…: Heb. made thee a million

segments.text: behold, thy time was the time of love; and I spread my skirt over thee, and covered thy nakedness: yea, I sware unto thee, and entered into a covenant with thee, and thou becamest mine.
segments.speaker: Lord GOD

segments.text: But thou didst trust in thine own beauty, and playedst the harlot because of thy renown, and pouredst out thy fornications on every one that passed by; his it was.
O harlot, hear the word of the LORD:
segments.speaker: Lord

segments.text: Behold, every one that useth proverbs shall use this proverb against thee, saying, As is the mother, so is her daughter.
Nevertheless I will remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth, and I will establish unto thee an everlasting covenant.
segments.speaker: Lord GOD

segments.text: And I will establish my covenant with thee; and thou shalt know that I am the LORD:
segments.speaker: Lord GOD

segments.text: That thou mayest remember, and be confounded, and never open thy mouth any more because of thy shame, when I am pacified toward thee for all that thou hast done.
segments.speaker: Lord GOD
